brief.title: Aeris
brief.work: Maintenance of the Website, and Application to track vehicles (Aertrak Connect)
brief.scope: Contributed to the maintenance of the Aeris website and the Aertrak Connect application, an IoT-based platform used to track vehicles, spanning both web and mobile (React Native) experiences.
brief.problem: Vehicle tracking requires reliable, real-time data representation across web and mobile platforms, with consistent UI behavior and business logic shared across both surfaces.
key_challenges: Maintaining consistent behavior and business logic across web (ReactJS) and mobile (React Native) applications.
Ensuring reliable data representation for real-time IoT vehicle tracking.
Supporting a native Android app (Aertrak) alongside the core web platform.
key_contributions: Maintained and enhanced UI components across web (React, MUI, Redux, Storybook) and mobile (React Native) codebases.
Worked with shared "React Logic" patterns to keep business rules consistent across platforms.
Supported testing (Jest, Enzyme) to maintain reliability of tracking features.
Collaborated with backend services connected to MySQL data sources for vehicle tracking data.
technical_focus: Real-time data rendering for IoT vehicle tracking.
Component reuse and shared logic across platforms.
brief.suggested_diagrams: Web-to-mobile shared logic architecture. IoT data flow from vehicle tracking to UI. Component reuse map (React ↔ React Native).
system_impact: Maintained platform reliability for a production IoT tracking product used across web and mobile.
Improved consistency between web and native app experiences.
Contributed to a more maintainable shared logic layer across platforms.
learnings: Building for IoT products requires designing interfaces that can represent real-time, sometimes imperfect data clearly and reliably. This experience deepened my understanding of maintaining consistency across web and native platforms sharing the same business logic.
